task_completion.description: Log into the OpenMRS Administration interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser showing the OpenMRS Administration dashboard after successfully logging in at https://localhost/openmrs/admin.

task_completion.description: Search for the target concept in the Concept Dictionary
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser showing the Concept Dictionary search page with 'Duplicate Diagnosis Code' entered in the search field.

task_completion.description: Open the edit page for the target concept
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser showing the concept details/edit page specifically for the concept named 'Duplicate Diagnosis Code'.
The text 'Administrative cleanup' is visible in the 'Retire Reason' text input field on the concept edit page.

task_completion.description: Save the changes to retire the concept
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message indicating the concept was saved successfully, or the concept's status clearly marked as 'Retired' (e.g., a checked 'Retired' box or status label).

integrity.description: Agent used a web browser to interact with the system
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a standard web browser interface (e.g., Chrome/Firefox) navigating the OpenMRS application, rather than terminal commands (like curl) or API testing tools.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the genuine OpenMRS application
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI matches the standard OpenMRS Administration web interface, not a text editor or a mock webpage.

integrity.description: Agent did not fake the success state using browser developer tools
integrity.visual_evidence: The success message or retired status appears consistent with a genuine page reload/save action, without open developer tools modifying the DOM.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target concept to be retired is exactly 'Duplicate Diagnosis Code'. The required retirement reason is 'Administrative cleanup' (the system verifies the presence of both 'administrative' and 'cleanup' in a case-insensitive manner). The system must be accessed via the OpenMRS admin interface at https://localhost/openmrs/admin.
